reasoning: The baseline price for the question is 138.79 (the market close on May 9, 2025, which corresponds to the May 11 weekend resolution). CRL experienced a massive structural hit in early April when the FDA announced a phase-out of animal testing, causing shares to plunge from the $160s to the $100s. The recent surge to 138.79 on May 9 was driven by Q1 earnings, massive share buybacks, and the announcement of a strategic review and board shakeup. However, this short-squeeze/activist premium appears to have peaked on May 8th (hitting 141.36) before pulling back on May 9th. The fundamental impairment from the FDA ruling and declining organic revenue suggest this elevated price will act as a ceiling, though a strategic review could provide a floor. Given the linear model prediction of a 0.45 probability to exceed the threshold and fading momentum, I am assigning a slight edge to a pullback in the short term, with probabilities reverting to 0.5 for longer horizons as uncertainty grows.
